from erk_shared.core.fakes import FakeLlmCaller
from erk_shared.core.llm_caller import LlmCallFailed, LlmResponse
from erk_shared.gateway.branch_manager.types import SubmitBranchError","new_string":"from erk.cli.commands.pr import pr_group
from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import PromptResult
from erk_shared.gateway.branch_manager.types import SubmitBranchError"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py","old_string":"def _make_rewrite_fakes(
    env: ErkIsolatedFsEnv,
    *,
    branch_name: str,
    pr_number: int = 42,
    pr_body: str = "",
    commits_ahead: int = 3,
    squash_raises: Exception | None = None,
    prompt_output: str = "Add awesome feature\n\nThis PR adds an awesome new feature.",
    prompt_error: str | None = None,
    submit_branch_error: SubmitBranchError | None = None,
) -> tuple[FakeGit, FakeGraphite, FakeGitHub, FakePromptExecutor, FakeLlmCaller]:
    """Create standard fakes for rewrite command tests."""
    pr_details = _make_pr_details(number=pr_number, branch=branch_name, body=pr_body)

    git = FakeGit(
        git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
        repository_roots={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
        local_branches={env.cwd: ["main", branch_name]},
        default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
        trunk_branches={env.git_dir: "main"},
        current_branches={env.cwd: branch_name},
        commits_ahead={(env.cwd, "main"): commits_ahead},
        diff_to_branch={(env.cwd, "main"): "diff --git a/file.py b/file.py\n+new content"},
    )

    graphite = FakeGraphite(
        authenticated=True,
        squash_branch_raises=squash_raises,
        branches={
            branch_name: BranchMetadata(
                name=branch_name,
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
            "main": BranchMetadata(
                name="main",
                parent=None,
                children=[branch_name],
                is_trunk=True,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    github = FakeGitHub(
        authenticated=True,
        prs_by_branch={branch_name: pr_details},
    )

    executor_kwargs: dict = {"available": True}
    if prompt_error is not None:
        executor_kwargs["simulated_prompt_error"] = prompt_error
    else:
        executor_kwargs["simulated_prompt_output"] = prompt_output
    executor = FakePromptExecutor(**executor_kwargs)

    if prompt_error is not None:
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmCallFailed(message=prompt_error))
    else:
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text=prompt_output))

    return git, graphite, github, executor, llm_caller","new_string":"def _make_rewrite_fakes(
    env: ErkIsolatedFsEnv,
    *,
    branch_name: str,
    pr_number: int = 42,
    pr_body: str = "",
    commits_ahead: int = 3,
    squash_raises: Exception | None = None,
    prompt_output: str = "Add awesome feature\n\nThis PR adds an awesome new feature.",
    prompt_error: str | None = None,
    submit_branch_error: SubmitBranchError | None = None,
) -> tuple[FakeGit, FakeGraphite, FakeGitHub, FakePromptExecutor]:
    """Create standard fakes for rewrite command tests."""
    pr_details = _make_pr_details(number=pr_number, branch=branch_name, body=pr_body)

    git = FakeGit(
        git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
        repository_roots={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
        local_branches={env.cwd: ["main", branch_name]},
        default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
        trunk_branches={env.git_dir: "main"},
        current_branches={env.cwd: branch_name},
        commits_ahead={(env.cwd, "main"): commits_ahead},
        diff_to_branch={(env.cwd, "main"): "diff --git a/file.py b/file.py\n+new content"},
    )

    graphite = FakeGraphite(
        authenticated=True,
        squash_branch_raises=squash_raises,
        branches={
            branch_name: BranchMetadata(
                name=branch_name,
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
            "main": BranchMetadata(
                name="main",
                parent=None,
                children=[branch_name],
                is_trunk=True,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    github = FakeGitHub(
        authenticated=True,
        prs_by_branch={branch_name: pr_details},
    )

    # Configure FakePromptExecutor with PromptResult for commit message generation
    if prompt_error is not None:
        prompt_results = [PromptResult(success=False, output="", error=prompt_error)]
    else:
        prompt_results = [PromptResult(success=True, output=prompt_output, error=None)]

    executor = FakePromptExecutor(available=True, prompt_results=prompt_results)

    return git, graphite, github, executor"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py","old_string":"def _make_rewrite_fakes(
    env: ErkIsolatedFsEnv,
    *,
    branch_name: str,
    pr_number: int = 42,
    pr_body: str = "",
    commits_ahead: int = 3,
    squash_raises: Exception | None = None,
    prompt_output: str = "Add awesome feature\n\nThis PR adds an awesome new feature.",
    prompt_error: str | None = None,
    submit_branch_error: SubmitBranchError | None = None,
) -> tuple[FakeGit, FakeGraphite, FakeGitHub, FakePromptExecutor, FakeLlmCaller]:
    """Create standard fakes for rewrite command tests."""
    pr_details = _make_pr_details(number=pr_number, branch=branch_name, body=pr_body)

    git = FakeGit(
        git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
        repository_roots={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
        local_branches={env.cwd: ["main", branch_name]},
        default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
        trunk_branches={env.git_dir: "main"},
        current_branches={env.cwd: branch_name},
        commits_ahead={(env.cwd, "main"): commits_ahead},
        diff_to_branch={(env.cwd, "main"): "diff --git a/file.py b/file.py\n+new content"},
    )

    graphite = FakeGraphite(
        authenticated=True,
        squash_branch_raises=squash_raises,
        branches={
            branch_name: BranchMetadata(
                name=branch_name,
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
            "main": BranchMetadata(
                name="main",
                parent=None,
                children=[branch_name],
                is_trunk=True,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    github = FakeGitHub(
        authenticated=True,
        prs_by_branch={branch_name: pr_details},
    )

    executor_kwargs: dict = {"available": True}
    if prompt_error is not None:
        executor_kwargs["simulated_prompt_error"] = prompt_error
    else:
        executor_kwargs["simulated_prompt_output"] = prompt_output
    executor = FakePromptExecutor(**executor_kwargs)

    if prompt_error is not None:
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmCallFailed(message=prompt_error))
    else:
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text=prompt_output))

    return git, graphite, github, executor, llm_caller","new_string":"def _make_rewrite_fakes(
    env: ErkIsolatedFsEnv,
    *,
    branch_name: str,
    pr_number: int = 42,
    pr_body: str = "",
    commits_ahead: int = 3,
    squash_raises: Exception | None = None,
    prompt_output: str = "Add awesome feature\n\nThis PR adds an awesome new feature.",
    prompt_error: str | None = None,
    submit_branch_error: SubmitBranchError | None = None,
) -> tuple[FakeGit, FakeGraphite, FakeGitHub, FakePromptExecutor]:
    """Create standard fakes for rewrite command tests."""
    pr_details = _make_pr_details(number=pr_number, branch=branch_name, body=pr_body)

    git = FakeGit(
        git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
        repository_roots={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
        local_branches={env.cwd: ["main", branch_name]},
        default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
        trunk_branches={env.git_dir: "main"},
        current_branches={env.cwd: branch_name},
        commits_ahead={(env.cwd, "main"): commits_ahead},
        diff_to_branch={(env.cwd, "main"): "diff --git a/file.py b/file.py\n+new content"},
    )

    graphite = FakeGraphite(
        authenticated=True,
        squash_branch_raises=squash_raises,
        branches={
            branch_name: BranchMetadata(
                name=branch_name,
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
            "main": BranchMetadata(
                name="main",
                parent=None,
                children=[branch_name],
                is_trunk=True,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    github = FakeGitHub(
        authenticated=True,
        prs_by_branch={branch_name: pr_details},
    )

    # Configure FakePromptExecutor with PromptResult for commit message generation
    if prompt_error is not None:
        prompt_results = [PromptResult(success=False, output="", error=prompt_error)]
    else:
        prompt_results = [PromptResult(success=True, output=prompt_output, error=None)]

    executor = FakePromptExecutor(available=True, prompt_results=prompt_results)

    return git, graphite, github, executor"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py","old_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_happy_path() -> None:
    """Test successful rewrite: squash, generate, amend, push, update PR."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        git, graphite, github, executor, llm_caller = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env, branch_name="feature"
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
        assert "PR title and description updated" in result.output
        assert "Add awesome feature" in result.output

        # Verify commit was amended
        assert len(git.commits) == 1
        commit_message = git.commits[0].message
        assert "Add awesome feature" in commit_message
        assert "awesome new feature" in commit_message

        # Verify branch was pushed (Graphite submit_stack was called)
        assert "Branch pushed" in result.output

        # Verify PR was updated
        assert len(github.updated_pr_titles) == 1
        assert github.updated_pr_titles[0] == (42, "Add awesome feature")","new_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_happy_path() -> None:
    """Test successful rewrite: squash, generate, amend, push, update PR."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        git, graphite, github, executor = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env, branch_name="feature"
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
        assert "PR title and description updated" in result.output
        assert "Add awesome feature" in result.output

        # Verify commit was amended
        assert len(git.commits) == 1
        commit_message = git.commits[0].message
        assert "Add awesome feature" in commit_message
        assert "awesome new feature" in commit_message

        # Verify branch was pushed (Graphite submit_stack was called)
        assert "Branch pushed" in result.output

        # Verify PR was updated
        assert len(github.updated_pr_titles) == 1
        assert github.updated_pr_titles[0] == (42, "Add awesome feature")"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py","old_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_already_single_commit() -> None:
    """Test rewrite with already-single-commit (squash is idempotent)."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        git, graphite, github, executor, llm_caller = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env, branch_name="feature", commits_ahead=1
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
        assert "PR title and description updated" in result.output","new_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_already_single_commit() -> None:
    """Test rewrite with already-single-commit (squash is idempotent)."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        git, graphite, github, executor = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env, branch_name="feature", commits_ahead=1
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
        assert "PR title and description updated" in result.output"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py","old_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_fails_when_squash_conflicts() -> None:
    """Test that rewrite fails when squash encounters conflicts."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        git, graphite, github, executor, llm_caller = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env,
            branch_name="feature",
            squash_raises=RuntimeError("Squash conflict: resolve manually"),
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code != 0
        assert "Squash failed" in result.output","new_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_fails_when_squash_conflicts() -> None:
    """Test that rewrite fails when squash encounters conflicts."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        git, graphite, github, executor = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env,
            branch_name="feature",
            squash_raises=RuntimeError("Squash conflict: resolve manually"),
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code != 0
        assert "Squash failed" in result.output"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py","old_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_fails_when_ai_generation_fails() -> None:
    """Test that rewrite fails when commit message generation fails."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        git, graphite, github, executor, llm_caller = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env,
            branch_name="feature",
            prompt_error="Claude CLI execution failed",
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code != 0
        assert "Failed to generate message" in result.output","new_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_fails_when_ai_generation_fails() -> None:
    """Test that rewrite fails when commit message generation fails."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        git, graphite, github, executor = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env,
            branch_name="feature",
            prompt_error="Claude CLI execution failed",
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code != 0
        assert "Failed to generate message" in result.output"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py","old_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_planned_pr_backend_preserves_metadata() -> None:
    """Test that rewrite preserves metadata prefix and omits Closes # for draft PR backend."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        metadata_body = format_plan_header_body_for_test()
        plan_content = "# My Plan\n\nImplement the thing."
        pr_body = build_plan_stage_body(metadata_body, plan_content, summary=None)

        git, graphite, github, executor, llm_caller = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env,
            branch_name="feature",
            pr_body=pr_body,
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
            plan_store=PlannedPRBackend(github, github.issues, time=FakeTime()),
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output

        # Verify the PR body preserves metadata prefix
        assert len(github.updated_pr_bodies) == 1
        updated_body = github.updated_pr_bodies[0][1]
        assert "plan-header" in updated_body
        # No self-closing reference for draft PR backend
        assert "Closes #" not in updated_body","new_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_planned_pr_backend_preserves_metadata() -> None:
    """Test that rewrite preserves metadata prefix and omits Closes # for draft PR backend."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        metadata_body = format_plan_header_body_for_test()
        plan_content = "# My Plan\n\nImplement the thing."
        pr_body = build_plan_stage_body(metadata_body, plan_content, summary=None)

        git, graphite, github, executor = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env,
            branch_name="feature",
            pr_body=pr_body,
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            plan_store=PlannedPRBackend(github, github.issues, time=FakeTime()),
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output

        # Verify the PR body preserves metadata prefix
        assert len(github.updated_pr_bodies) == 1
        updated_body = github.updated_pr_bodies[0][1]
        assert "plan-header" in updated_body
        # No self-closing reference for draft PR backend
        assert "Closes #" not in updated_body"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py","old_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_retracks_graphite_branch_after_amend() -> None:
    """Test that rewrite retracks Graphite branch immediately after amending commit.

    The retrack must happen right after amend (before push or PR update) so that
    Graphite's cache stays consistent even if subsequent operations fail.
    """
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        git, graphite, github, executor, llm_caller = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env, branch_name="feature"
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
            use_graphite=True,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
        # Verify retrack_branch was called with repo root and branch name
        assert len(graphite.retrack_branch_calls) == 1
        retrack_cwd, retrack_branch = graphite.retrack_branch_calls[0]
        assert retrack_cwd == env.git_dir
        assert retrack_branch == "feature"","new_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_retracks_graphite_branch_after_amend() -> None:
    """Test that rewrite retracks Graphite branch immediately after amending commit.

    The retrack must happen right after amend (before push or PR update) so that
    Graphite's cache stays consistent even if subsequent operations fail.
    """
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        git, graphite, github, executor = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env, branch_name="feature"
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            use_graphite=True,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output
        # Verify retrack_branch was called with repo root and branch name
        assert len(graphite.retrack_branch_calls) == 1
        retrack_cwd, retrack_branch = graphite.retrack_branch_calls[0]
        assert retrack_cwd == env.git_dir
        assert retrack_branch == "feature""}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py","old_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_skips_lifecycle_when_plan_not_resolved() -> None:
    """Rewrite skips lifecycle update when plan cannot be resolved from branch name."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        # plnd/ branch — resolve_plan_id_for_branch returns None
        branch_name = "plnd/add-feature"

        # Create plan issue with lifecycle_stage "planned"
        plan_body = format_plan_header_body_for_test(lifecycle_stage="planned")
        plan_issue = IssueInfo(
            number=100,
            title="Plan #100",
            body=plan_body,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/100",
            labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"],
            assignees=[],
            created_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15, 10, 30, tzinfo=UTC),
            updated_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15, 10, 30, tzinfo=UTC),
            author="test-user",
        )
        fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={100: plan_issue})

        git, graphite, github, executor, llm_caller = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env,
            branch_name=branch_name,
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
            issues=fake_issues,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output

        # Plan cannot be resolved from branch name — no lifecycle update
        assert len(fake_issues.updated_bodies) == 0","new_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_skips_lifecycle_when_plan_not_resolved() -> None:
    """Rewrite skips lifecycle update when plan cannot be resolved from branch name."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        # plnd/ branch — resolve_plan_id_for_branch returns None
        branch_name = "plnd/add-feature"

        # Create plan issue with lifecycle_stage "planned"
        plan_body = format_plan_header_body_for_test(lifecycle_stage="planned")
        plan_issue = IssueInfo(
            number=100,
            title="Plan #100",
            body=plan_body,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/100",
            labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"],
            assignees=[],
            created_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15, 10, 30, tzinfo=UTC),
            updated_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15, 10, 30, tzinfo=UTC),
            author="test-user",
        )
        fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={100: plan_issue})

        git, graphite, github, executor = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env,
            branch_name=branch_name,
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            issues=fake_issues,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output

        # Plan cannot be resolved from branch name — no lifecycle update
        assert len(fake_issues.updated_bodies) == 0"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py","old_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_skips_lifecycle_when_plan_not_resolved() -> None:
    """Rewrite skips lifecycle update when plan cannot be resolved from branch name."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        # plnd/ branch — resolve_plan_id_for_branch returns None
        branch_name = "plnd/add-feature"

        # Create plan issue with lifecycle_stage "planned"
        plan_body = format_plan_header_body_for_test(lifecycle_stage="planned")
        plan_issue = IssueInfo(
            number=100,
            title="Plan #100",
            body=plan_body,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/100",
            labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"],
            assignees=[],
            created_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15, 10, 30, tzinfo=UTC),
            updated_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15, 10, 30, tzinfo=UTC),
            author="test-user",
        )
        fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={100: plan_issue})

        git, graphite, github, executor, llm_caller = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env,
            branch_name=branch_name,
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
            issues=fake_issues,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output

        # Plan cannot be resolved from branch name — no lifecycle update
        assert len(fake_issues.updated_bodies) == 0","new_string":"def test_pr_rewrite_skips_lifecycle_when_plan_not_resolved() -> None:
    """Rewrite skips lifecycle update when plan cannot be resolved from branch name."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        # plnd/ branch — resolve_plan_id_for_branch returns None
        branch_name = "plnd/add-feature"

        # Create plan issue with lifecycle_stage "planned"
        plan_body = format_plan_header_body_for_test(lifecycle_stage="planned")
        plan_issue = IssueInfo(
            number=100,
            title="Plan #100",
            body=plan_body,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/100",
            labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"],
            assignees=[],
            created_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15, 10, 30, tzinfo=UTC),
            updated_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15, 10, 30, tzinfo=UTC),
            author="test-user",
        )
        fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={100: plan_issue})

        git, graphite, github, executor = _make_rewrite_fakes(
            env,
            branch_name=branch_name,
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            issues=fake_issues,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["rewrite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0, result.output

        # Plan cannot be resolved from branch name — no lifecycle update
        assert len(fake_issues.updated_bodies) == 0"}

session.assistant: The remaining agents are still working on the larger test files. Let me also check if there's a custom `FakePromptExecutor` in tests/fakes that might have incompatible kwargs:
session.user:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below: Analysis: Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:
1. The user invoked `/erk:plan-implement 8874` which is a command to implement plan #8874.
2. The plan was set up via `erk exec setup-impl --issue 8874` which created an implementation directory.
3. I read the plan at `.erk/impl-context/plnd--flat-prompt-executors-03-06-1518/plan.md` - this plan is about "Flat prompt executor namespace + kill LlmCaller".
4. I loaded coding standards (dignified-python skill) and read the minimum Python version (3.10).
5. I read all the critical source files to understand the current architecture: - `src/erk/core/prompt_executor.py` - ClaudePromptExecutor (the real Claude CLI executor) - `src/erk/core/codex_prompt_executor.py` - CodexPromptExecutor - `src/erk/core/context.py` - Application context with dependency injection - `src/erk/core/fast_llm.py` - AnthropicLlmCaller (thin wrapper over PromptExecutor) - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/llm_caller.py` - LlmCaller ABC - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/fakes.py` - FakeLlmCaller, FakePromptExecutor - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/context.py` - ErkContext dataclass - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/testing.py` - Test factory - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/factories.py` - Production factory - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/prompt_executor.py` - PromptExecutor ABC - `src/erk/core/commit_message_generator.py` - Uses LlmCaller - `src/erk/cli/commands/objective/plan_cmd.py` - Uses ctx.llm_caller - `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_dispatch.py` - Uses ctx.llm_caller.call() - `src/erk/core/branch_slug_generator.py` - Uses PromptExecutor
6. I created a TodoWrite with 9 steps.
7. Steps 1-2: Renamed ClaudePromptExecutor → ClaudeCliPromptExecutor and CodexPromptExecutor → CodexCliPromptExecutor using libcst-refactor subagents (completed successfully).
8. Steps 3-4: Created two new files: - `src/erk/core/anthropic_prompt_executor.py` - AnthropicApiPromptExecutor - `src/erk/core/fallback_prompt_executor.py` - FallbackPromptExecutor
9. Step 5: Updated `src/erk/core/context.py` to wire FallbackPromptExecutor, removed llm_caller from context_for_test and create_context.
10. Step 6: Killed LlmCaller: - Deleted `src/erk/core/fast_llm.py` - Deleted `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/llm_caller.py` - Removed llm_caller field from ErkContext dataclass - Removed FakeLlmCaller and LlmCall from fakes.py - Updated testing.py and factories.py
11. Step 7: Updated callers: - `commit_message_generator.py` - Changed from LlmCaller to PromptExecutor - `submit_pipeline.py`, `rewrite_cmd.py`, `update_pr_description.py` - Changed `ctx.llm_caller` to `ctx.prompt_executor` - `one_shot_dispatch.py` - Replaced direct `ctx.llm_caller.call()` with `generate_branch_slug(ctx.prompt_executor, ...)` - `plan_cmd.py` - Updated `_generate_slug` to use PromptExecutor
12. Step 8: Tests update - launched 5 background agents: - test_commit_message_generator.py (agent a6cf9b8) - still running - test_submit.py (agent a293280) - still running - test_rewrite.py (agent aca5f05) - still running - test_update_pr_description.py (agent aa0310b) - COMPLETED - test_submit_graphite_disabled.py (agent a5915cd) - COMPLETED - Also deleted test_fast_llm.py
13. Just before the summary was requested, I was checking for a tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py custom FakePromptExecutor, and 3 of the 5 agents were still running (a6cf9b8, a293280, aca5f05).
Key observations: - The FakePromptExecutor in tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py uses different kwargs (`available`, `simulated_prompt_output`, `simulated_prompt_error`) vs the standard FakePromptExecutor in erk_shared/core/fakes.py which uses (`is_available`, `prompt_results`). The subagents may have issues with this. - The test_submit.py agent was using kwargs like `available=True, simulated_prompt_output="..."` which are NOT parameters of the standard FakePromptExecutor but ARE parameters of the tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py custom one. This could be a problem since the agents were told to use `prompt_results` parameter which is from the standard FakePromptExecutor. - Actually looking at the agent outputs more carefully, the test_submit.py and test_submit_graphite_disabled.py agents were using both `available=True` AND `prompt_results=[...]` together, which seems like they're mixing the custom FakePromptExecutor's `available` kwarg with the standard one's `prompt_results`. Need to verify this works.
Let me check what the test agents actually did vs what's needed. The agents seem to have made edits, but the results show they used `prompt_results` parameter. The custom FakePromptExecutor at tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py may or may not accept `prompt_results`. This is a potential issue.
Pending tasks: - Step 8 is in progress: 3 agents (commit message gen tests, submit tests, rewrite tests) are still running - Step 9: Run CI and fix issues (not started) - Need to write new tests for AnthropicApiPromptExecutor and FallbackPromptExecutor - After CI: submit PR via `erk pr submit`
Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user invoked `/erk:plan-implement 8874` to implement plan #8874: "Flat prompt executor namespace + kill LlmCaller". The plan's goal is to: - Rename `ClaudePromptExecutor` → `ClaudeCliPromptExecutor` and `CodexPromptExecutor` → `CodexCliPromptExecutor` - Create `AnthropicApiPromptExecutor` (direct Anthropic SDK calls) and `FallbackPromptExecutor` (API-first, CLI-fallback composite) - Wire `FallbackPromptExecutor` in `create_context()` so `ctx.prompt_executor.execute_prompt()` tries the API first - Delete `LlmCaller` ABC, `AnthropicLlmCaller`, `FakeLlmCaller`, and `fast_llm.py` - Update all callers (commit message generator, branch slug generator, one-shot dispatch, objective plan) to use `ctx.prompt_executor.execute_prompt()` instead of `ctx.llm_caller.call()` - Update all tests
2. Key Technical Concepts: - **PromptExecutor ABC** (`erk_shared.core.prompt_executor`): Abstract interface with methods `execute_prompt()`, `execute_command_streaming()`, `execute_interactive()`, `execute_prompt_passthrough()` - **FallbackPromptExecutor pattern**: Composite that routes `execute_prompt()` through API when available, falls back to CLI; all other methods always delegate to CLI - **FakePromptExecutor** (`erk_shared.core.fakes`): Test fake that accepts `prompt_results: list[PromptResult]` for queued responses - **Custom FakePromptExecutor** (`tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py`): Test-only version with different kwargs (`available`, `simulated_prompt_output`, `simulated_prompt_error`) - **PromptResult** dataclass: `success: bool`, `output: str`, `error: str | None` - **LlmCaller** (deleted): Was an ABC with `call()` returning `LlmResponse | NoApiKey | LlmCallFailed` - **context_for_test()**: Test factory at `src/erk/core/context.py` and `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/testing.py` - **Frozen dataclass pattern**: ErkContext is `@dataclass(frozen=True)` - Python 3.10 minimum version
3. Files and Code Sections:
- **`src/erk/core/prompt_executor.py`** - Contains `ClaudeCliPromptExecutor` (renamed from `ClaudePromptExecutor`). Real CLI-based executor.
- **`src/erk/core/codex_prompt_executor.py`** - Contains `CodexCliPromptExecutor` (renamed from `CodexPromptExecutor`). Codex CLI-based executor.
- **`src/erk/core/anthropic_prompt_executor.py`** - NEW FILE. Direct Anthropic SDK executor: ```python class AnthropicApiPromptExecutor(PromptExecutor): def is_available(self) -> bool: return os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY") is not None
def execute_prompt(self, prompt, *, model, tools, cwd, system_prompt, dangerous) -> PromptResult: # Uses Anthropic SDK directly client = Anthropic(api_key=api_key) response = client.messages.create(**kwargs) return PromptResult(success=True, output=content_block.text.strip(), error=None)
# execute_command_streaming, execute_interactive, execute_prompt_passthrough all raise NotImplementedError ```
- **`src/erk/core/fallback_prompt_executor.py`** - NEW FILE. Composite executor: ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class FallbackPromptExecutor(PromptExecutor): api_executor: PromptExecutor cli_executor: PromptExecutor
def execute_prompt(self, prompt, **kwargs) -> PromptResult: if self.api_executor.is_available(): return self.api_executor.execute_prompt(prompt, **kwargs) return self.cli_executor.execute_prompt(prompt, **kwargs)
# All other methods delegate directly to cli_executor ```
- **`src/erk/core/context.py`** - Updated wiring in `create_context()`: ```python # Old: llm_caller = AnthropicLlmCaller(prompt_executor=ClaudeCliPromptExecutor(console=None)) # New: cli_executor = create_prompt_executor(global_config=global_config, console=console) prompt_executor: PromptExecutor = FallbackPromptExecutor( api_executor=AnthropicApiPromptExecutor(), cli_executor=cli_executor, ) ``` Also removed `llm_caller` parameter from `context_for_test()` function, removed `FakeLlmCaller` and `LlmResponse` imports, removed `llm_caller` from all ErkContext construction calls.
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/context.py`** - Removed `llm_caller: LlmCaller` field from `ErkContext` dataclass. Removed `llm_caller` parameter from `for_test()` static method.
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/testing.py`** - Removed `llm_caller` parameter, `FakeLlmCaller` import, `resolved_llm_caller` variable.
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/factories.py`** - Removed `FakeLlmCaller` and `LlmResponse` imports, removed `llm_caller=` from ErkContext construction.
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/fakes.py`** - Removed `FakeLlmCaller` class, `LlmCall` namedtuple, and all `llm_caller` imports.
- **DELETED: `src/erk/core/fast_llm.py`** - Was `AnthropicLlmCaller` (thin wrapper). - **DELETED: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/llm_caller.py`** - Was `LlmCaller` ABC. - **DELETED: `tests/core/test_fast_llm.py`** - Tests for deleted fast_llm module.
- **`src/erk/core/commit_message_generator.py`** - Changed from LlmCaller to PromptExecutor: ```python # Old: def __init__(self, llm_caller: LlmCaller, *, time: Time) -> None: self._llm_caller = llm_caller # New: def __init__(self, prompt_executor: PromptExecutor, *, time: Time) -> None: self._executor = prompt_executor ``` Internal logic changed from `self._llm_caller.call()` returning `LlmResponse | NoApiKey | LlmCallFailed` to `self._executor.execute_prompt()` returning `PromptResult`. Error checking changed from `isinstance(result, (NoApiKey, LlmCallFailed))` to `not result.success`.
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_dispatch.py`** - Replaced inline `ctx.llm_caller.call()` with `generate_branch_slug(ctx.prompt_executor, params.prompt)`. Cleaned up imports (removed `BRANCH_SLUG_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `_postprocess_slug`, `LlmCallFailed`, `NoApiKey`).
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/objective/plan_cmd.py`** - Rewrote `_generate_slug()`: ```python # Old: def _generate_slug(llm_caller: LlmCaller, description: str) -> str # New: def _generate_slug(prompt_executor: PromptExecutor, description: str) -> str ``` Call sites changed from `ctx.llm_caller` to `ctx.prompt_executor`.
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py`**, **`rewrite_cmd.py`**, **`exec/scripts/update_pr_description.py`** - Changed `CommitMessageGenerator(ctx.llm_caller, time=ctx.time)` to `CommitMessageGenerator(ctx.prompt_executor, time=ctx.time)`.
- **`tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py`** - EXISTS but not yet read. This is a custom FakePromptExecutor used in some tests with different kwargs (`available`, `simulated_prompt_output`, `simulated_prompt_error`) than the standard one in `erk_shared.core.fakes`.
4. Errors and Fixes: - **`erk exec impl-signal started` failed** with `session-id-required` error because `$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` was not available in the command context. This was non-critical (the `|| true` handled it gracefully). - **Potential issue with test FakePromptExecutor kwargs**: The background agents updating test_submit.py and test_submit_graphite_disabled.py used kwargs like `available=True, prompt_results=[...]` which mix the custom FakePromptExecutor's `available` parameter with the standard one's `prompt_results`. The custom `tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py` file needs to be checked for compatibility - this is not yet verified.
5. Problem Solving: - Successfully eliminated the entire `LlmCaller` abstraction layer by routing all lightweight LLM calls through the existing `PromptExecutor.execute_prompt()` interface - The `FallbackPromptExecutor` composite pattern allows API-first execution without changing any caller code - The `CommitMessageGenerator` internal threading pattern was preserved but adapted from `LlmCaller.call()` to `PromptExecutor.execute_prompt()` - For `one_shot_dispatch.py`, replaced complex inline `ctx.llm_caller.call()` + error handling with the simpler `generate_branch_slug()` convenience function
6. All User Messages: - The user's only message was the `/erk:plan-implement 8874` command invocation, which triggered the entire implementation workflow. No additional user feedback was provided.
7. Pending Tasks: - **Step 8 (in progress)**: 3 background agents still running: - Agent `a6cf9b8`: Updating `tests/core/test_commit_message_generator.py` (was mid-edit, had completed ~10 of ~15 test function edits) - Agent `a293280`: Updating `tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py` (was mid-edit, had completed ~12 of ~16 test function edits) - Agent `aca5f05`: Updating `tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py` (appeared to have completed all edits, was verifying) - **Step 9**: Run CI and fix issues (not started) - **Need to verify**: `tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py` custom FakePromptExecutor compatibility with changes made by background agents - **Post-CI steps**: Clean up impl-context, submit PR via `erk pr submit`
8. Current Work: I was in Step 8 (Update tests) when the summary was requested. Five background Task agents were launched to update test files in parallel. Two completed successfully (`test_update_pr_description.py` and `test_submit_graphite_disabled.py`). Three were still running (`test_commit_message_generator.py`, `test_submit.py`, `test_rewrite.py`).
I had just checked for the existence of `tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py` (a custom FakePromptExecutor) via `Glob` and confirmed it exists. I had not yet read this file to verify compatibility with the changes being made by the background agents.
The last action before the summary request was: ``` Glob pattern: tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py Result: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py ```
9. Optional Next Step: Wait for the 3 remaining background agents to complete (agents `a6cf9b8`, `a293280`, `aca5f05`), then read `tests/fakes/prompt_executor.py` to verify the custom FakePromptExecutor is compatible with the changes. After all test updates are complete, run CI (Step 9) to catch any remaining compilation or test failures, then iterate to fix them. This is directly in line with the plan-implement workflow's Step 8 → Step 9 sequence.. Please continue the conversation from where we left it off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.
session.assistant: I need to continue with Step 8 (updating tests) and then move to Step 9 (CI). Let me first check the status of the background agents and read the custom FakePromptExecutor to understand compatibility.
